license_label: Washington State UBI Number (Unified Business Identifier) / Contractor Registration Number
license_notes: Washington requires ALL contractors to register with the Dept. of Labor and Industries and be bonded and insured. Electrical, plumbing, asbestos, elevators, construction cranes require separate state licenses. Unregistered = gross misdemeanor; no lien rights.

mandatory_clauses.label: Washington contractor registration disclosure
Bond and insurance disclosure
Washington lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE TO CUSTOMER (RCW 18.27.114)

Under Washington state law (RCW 60.04), your contractor, subcontractors, and suppliers of materials may have a claim against your property if not paid. This claim is known as a construction lien. This lien claim could result in loss of your property. We encourage you to protect yourself by:
1. Requiring your contractor to supply you with an 'Informational Notice to Customer' from each subcontractor and supplier;
2. Not making a final payment until you are satisfied all people who have worked on or provided materials for your home have been paid.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: Gross misdemeanor for unregistered contractors. No lien rights for unregistered contractors. DLI regularly inspects job sites.
notes: Washington requires registration (not just a license) from ALL contractors. Must be bonded and insured. GCs that hire subcontractors must register as a general contractor (specialty contractor designation cannot subcontract). Electrical, plumbing, asbestos, elevators, cranes require separate licenses. Seattle has no business license requirement for most contractors. Portland/Vancouver area has additional requirements. Unregistered = no lien rights AND gross misdemeanor.
